key_insight: A Puck-informed phase-field fatigue model with separate fibre-dominated and matrix/inter-fibre channels can identify the active damage mode in unidirectional composites while capturing crack evolution and stiffness loss.
engineering_relevance: The framework helps predict mode-specific fatigue damage across fibre angles and geometries, supporting more reliable composite design without re-tuning for each case.
method: Uses two scalar phase-field damage channels: one for fibre-dominated fatigue and one for matrix/inter-fibre fatigue.
Assigns each channel its own fatigue history variable, threshold, and fracture-resistance degradation law.
Implements the model in Abaqus/Standard via a compact UMAT-UEL with one orthotropic constitutive law plus two phase-field layers.
evidence_signal: Verified with one-element tests, parameter sweeps, and centred-notch/open-hole tension simulations at 0°, 45°, and 90° under monotonic and cyclic loading, reproducing the expected crack modes and life ordering.
watch_item: The study is a numerical verification rather than a calibrated experimental life-prediction claim, so transferability to new materials or loading regimes still needs validation.
paper_title: A Puck-informed mode-resolved phase-field fatigue framework for unidirectional composites
